key_insight: Open-source smaller LLMs can support privacy-preserving assessment of shared decision making, but they still need human oversight because accuracy and grounding remain limited.
clinical_relevance: A locally deployable model could help analyze clinical consultations without sending sensitive transcript data to external services, potentially supporting scalable SDM quality review.
method: Compared 3 general-domain and 2 medical-domain open-source smaller LLMs.
Used Dutch melanoma consultation transcripts annotated by experts with the OPTION12 framework.
Tested a Judge-LLM consensus approach to resolve model disagreement.
evidence_signal: In a development-phase pilot on expert-annotated consultations, Gemma3:12b performed best, reaching Pearson r=0.51 and Spearman ρ=0.59 against human ratings.
watch_item: Medical-domain models showed hallucinations and instruction-following failures, so these systems are not ready to replace human annotators.
paper_title: Measuring the practice of shared-decision making (OPTION12): An Investigation into Open-sourced Smaller LLMs (OS-sLLMs) for Better Privacy and Sustainability
